title: viftyctl run --json report
description: Machine-readable report emitted by viftyctl run --json after the child command has launched and exited. Pre-child failures still use the command-error schema.

childTerminationReason.description: Best-effort classification for the wrapped child result. `exited` means no shell-style signal mapping was inferred from childExitCode; `signalInferred` means childExitCode looked like 128 + signal. Omitted in legacy reports.

childSignal.description: Signal number inferred from a shell-style childExitCode when childTerminationReason is signalInferred. Omitted otherwise and in legacy reports.

childSignalName.description: Short signal name such as INT, TERM, or HUP when Vifty recognizes the inferred childSignal. Omitted otherwise and in legacy reports.

resolvedChildExecutable.description: Absolute executable path selected by viftyctl run child-command resolution before the cooling lease was requested. Present in current builds when runLifecycle.resolvedChildExecutableReported is true; absent in legacy run reports.

resolvedChildExecutableSHA256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 digest of the resolved executable bytes, computed after child-command resolution and before the cooling lease is requested when the executable file is readable. Omitted when unavailable or in legacy run reports.

resolvedChildExecutableSHA256Status.description: Digest availability status for the resolved executable in current run reports. `computed` means resolvedChildExecutableSHA256 contains the lowercase SHA-256 digest; `unavailable` means Vifty could not read or hash the executable bytes. Omitted in legacy run reports.
